concept.core_loop: One-thumb: drag to glide a small forest-spirit around an autumn glade. Each second you choose a path — dart toward glowing seeds (score + a streak combo) or peel away from roaming thorn-sprite hazards that drift toward you. Collecting seeds without being hit builds a streak that boosts score; one hit ends the run (tap to restart). Difficulty ramps every ~12s: hazards spawn more often and drift faster, up to a cap. The live decision is risk/reward pathing — grab the seed tucked next to a hazard, or play safe.
mechanics: streak/combo reward beat (consecutive seeds without a hit -> score multiplier)
difficulty ramp (spawn rate + hazard speed tiers, capped)
game over on hazard contact + tap-to-restart
concept.art_direction: Representational PAINTERLY/illustrated forest creatures — this is the M1.5 RASTER target (NOT geometric/SVG): a soft rounded forest-spirit, organic thorn-sprite hazards, glowing seed-motes, an autumn-forest backdrop. Builder placeholder (primitives, to be raster re-skinned later): warm autumn palette — amber/rust/gold creatures and bright gold seed-circles on a deep forest-green field; soft rounded blobs for the spirit and hazards (organic, not sharp/geometric); parallax bands of darker tree-silhouette shapes drifting behind for depth; feedback beats: gentle warm pulse + scale-pop on seed pickup, red flash + screen shake on a hit.
concept.differentiation_notes: Not a third runner and not the top-down shooter: a calm-but-tense forage with risk/reward pathing and no weapons. The real hook is being the set's first REPRESENTATIONAL/painterly title (M1.5 raster) — its creatures and foliage are exactly the art SVG does badly, so it is the right proof target for the raster method.

theme.premise: a cozy autumn-woodland folktale of a small forest spirit foraging glowing seeds
theme.setting: dappled autumn forest at golden hour

issues: asset: re-skin aesthetic judged "very bad" by owner (styled A/B, 2026-06-01). Root cause CLARIFIED (owner, retroactively): same within-game visual INCOHESION as crosser — generated sprites do not read as one design language/world — PLUS the background was never re-skinned (asset_pass.recipes=[spirit,hazard] only; BG is primitive flat bands). Individually rendered but low cohesion. Owner deferred fix. FIX: asset SKILL.md needs a shared world/character design language from concept.theme + a background treatment.

audio: SFX character wrong for theme (audio A/B 2026-06-01) — SFX are electronic/explosive while art + concept.theme are natural/cozy (warm, gentle, organic, storybook). Audio skill derived an aggressive electronic palette contradicting concept.theme.tone. Confirmed by the crosser contrast (same palette wrong there too) = the skill is not theme-bound. FIX: audio SKILL.md must derive SFX sonic character from concept.theme.

audio: BGM CONFIRMED NOT PLAYING (probe 2026-06-01: MusicAmbient.playing=false, pos=0 after 90 frames). CORRECTS earlier buried-bed guess — the bed never starts. ROOT CAUSE: play() called synchronously inside _ready right after add_child (_setup_audio Main.gd:164-167), which does not reliably start an AudioStreamPlayer that frame; SFX work because they fire later. (Secondary: bgm.wav is also mixed ~13 dB under SFX at -27 dBFS RMS — fix levels too.) FIX: audio SKILL.md should use autoplay=true or a deferred/awaited play() for the bed, and balance music vs SFX.

visual_system.world_bible: A hand-painted autumn-woodland storybook folktale — warm gouache, soft inner glow, the look of a single cozy children's picture book. ONE world: a dappled autumn forest at golden hour. ONE character family: every actor is a small, round, soft-bodied woodland sprite-creature rendered in the same painterly medium and warm amber/rust/gold palette, lit by the same soft magical glow, so hero and hazard read as inhabitants of the same book. The hero is a gentle amber seed-spirit (soft, round, friendly); the hazard is its dark sibling — a thorn-sprite of the same forest, the same painterly storybook rendering but coated in dark charcoal brambles and rust-red thorns with glowing eyes, a bristling spiky silhouette so threat reads at a glance. Distinguished by material + silhouette + value (soft/warm/light vs spiky/dark), unified by world + medium + palette + lighting — never two different worlds (the A/B-round-1 incohesion finding).
visual_system.form: soft rounded painterly storybook creatures; deliberate silhouette contrast between the smooth round amber hero and the spiky bristled dark hazard so threat reads at a glance
visual_system.shading: painterly soft-brushwork with soft diffuse magical lighting on the creatures; flat dark procedural field + parallax tree silhouettes behind
visual_system.scale: 1024 square masters for spirit + hazard, downscaled at runtime to ~110px / ~92px footprints; mipmaps on, LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS filtering, lossless compression (2 sprites)
visual_system.prompt_scaffold: painterly storybook illustration, soft hand-painted brushwork, cozy childrens book art, warm autumn woodland palette of amber rust and gold, soft diffuse magical lighting, single centered subject, full body, clean transparent background, high detail,

style.style_prompt: painterly storybook illustration, soft hand-painted brushwork, cozy childrens book art

asset_pass.notes: A/B-ROUND-3 ART RE-PASS (2026-06-02, GPU-validated). Addresses the round-2 owner re-playtest verdict ('art terrible/not great'). KEY REFRAME (round-3 probe): the SPRITES were already fine; 'terrible' came from (1) a flat primitive background (dark-green void + parallax silhouettes) and (2) the hero too small in a big field — NOT sprite quality. Fixes: (a) NEW themed BACKGROUND generation (the M1.7-deferred capability, now in scope) — a full-frame OPAQUE painterly autumn-glade via the plain sdxl template (layerdiffuse:false, 768x1344 portrait, seed 701); drawn as the bottom layer in _draw_background (guarded, procedural fallback kept), replacing the flat void. In-frame screenshot confirms a rich golden-hour forest backdrop with the spirit compositing cleanly (no alpha halo, no double-draw). (b) HERO SIZING: SPIRIT_DRAW 110->150 px (visual only; hitbox SPIRIT_RADIUS=26 untouched) so the spirit reads as the subject. (c) REFINE-PASS VALIDATION (the UNPROVEN structural concern): generated a spirit via the sdxl-layerdiffuse-refine template (1.5x LatentUpscale + denoise-0.45 second pass -> 1536 master) and objectively verified the LayerDiffuse alpha-join stays CLEAN on the upscaled latent (Godot alpha check: transparent corners 0.00, transFrac 0.61 == the 1024 original's 0.62, equivalent edge). So refine is now GPU-VERIFIED-CLEAN, but per the round-3 low-ROI reframe it was NOT adopted as the shipped spirit (marginal gain + would churn the store icon/atlas derived from spirit.png); kept as a validated lever. Sprites (spirit, hazard) UNCHANGED from round-2. selftest SELFTEST OK; headless import clean; logic untouched. Owner aesthetic A/B pending (Phase-2 playtest) — technical validation done, aesthetic sign-off is the remaining gate. --- PRIOR (A/B-ROUND-2 RE-SKIN, 2026-06-01) — addresses the round-1 'very bad / incohesion' finding by deriving a world_bible FIRST (see visual_system.world_bible) and regenerating the hazard as the spirit's dark sibling in the SAME painterly storybook world, instead of an unrelated darker critter. spirit KEPT (round-1 pass-1 amber seed-spirit, already on-bible). hazard REGENERATED: round-1 produced a dark spiky bird holding a stick (off-family, prop, different read); round-2 hazard is a dark charcoal+rust-red thorn-creature with glowing eyes and a bristling spiky silhouette that reads as danger at a glance while sharing the spirit's painterly medium + autumn palette + soft glow (cohesion via world/medium/palette/lighting, contrast via material/silhouette/value). Hazard iteration (4 passes, the cozy-storybook scaffold is a strong 'cute mammal' attractor): fox-in-a-jacket -> cute bird-on-branch -> [KEPT] dark red-eyed thorn-creature -> cute hamster-with-pumpkin; attempt 3 (seed 352) is the only one whose threat reads at a glance, so it was restored. Style A (painterly/warm-woodland) via ComfyUI + SDXL Juggernaut v9 + LayerDiffuse (RGBA); both sprites drawn via draw_texture_rect in the existing immediate-mode _draw() (z-order/shake/squash preserved). MIXED-METHOD unchanged: seed stays a primitive glow-mote (round-1: SDXL over-elaborated a 14px dot across 4 tries; procedural glow is higher-quality). BACKGROUND: parallax autumn tree-silhouette field stays PROCEDURAL and is listed in left_primitive — a KNOWN M1.7-DEFERRED cohesion gap (static-themed-backdrop generation waits for M1.7), not a silent omission. Visual-verified by composing a play frame with the SceneTree screenshot harness (real Vulkan renderer, RTX 5080) — spirit, hazard, seeds, backdrop, HUD all render with no double-draw. Selftest SELFTEST OK; headless run clean; logic untouched. STATUS HELD AT validated (validated->styled is illegal): awaits owner ~60s playtest -> playable, then human A/B + IP-safety -> styled. IP-safety: generic descriptors only, no franchise/character/studio names; every recipe negative excludes logo/watermark/text/trademarked-character/brand/celebrity.

audio_system.sonic_character: warm wooden KALIMBA / thumb-piano pluck (mbira) — soft organic rounded notes, gentle warm taps, NO electronic/synth transients, NO explosive or harsh attacks. Round-3 lesson: generic 'bell chime'/music-box/celesta read as 'irritating tings'; naming a concrete warm instrument (kalimba) is the cozy winner. SFX draw their material from this vocabulary (the audio sibling of the visual world_bible); the deterministic envelope (comfy.mjs envelopeSfxWav) shapes loudness, and steps 50-100 (not 8) keep the tone clean instead of broadband-noisy.

audio_pass.notes: A/B-ROUND-3 AUDIO RE-PASS (2026-06-02, GPU-validated calibration). Addresses the round-2 owner re-playtest verdict (SFX still 'explosive', BGM 'weak'). ROOT CAUSES found empirically (docs/superpowers/2026-06-02-audio-art-probe-results.md): (a) explosive SFX = generation params not prompt — steps≈8 under-denoises to broadband noise (ZCR up to 8.9k/s); steps 50-100 = clean tone. Regenerated all 3 SFX at steps 55 cfg 6 (objective check: ZCR 740-1205/s = clean, vs round-2's noise). (b) SFX needed a deterministic loudness/trim envelope — now AUTO-APPLIED by comfy.mjs genAudio for kind:sfx (envelopeSfxWav: trim-to-event -> loudness-normalize RMS 0.13 / 0.97 peak clamp -> fade 6/40ms); no hand-application. (c) cozy timbre must name a concrete warm instrument: switched to KALIMBA/thumb-piano (generic bell-chime read as irritating tings). (d) BGM ambient/pad prompt collapsed to a DRONE; fix = force a plucked MELODY (fingerpicked nylon-guitar lullaby, low/warm register) + aggressive anti-drone + anti-high-pitch negatives + cfg 8 (winning probe seed 311). LEVELS re-balanced for the new beds: bed is a quiet lullaby (~RMS 0.037), so volume_db RAISED to +2 (round-2 was -4, which buried it) to sit audible-but-under the SFX. Wiring (bed rebuild + autoplay) unchanged from round-2 (still correct). Owner ear-judgment pending (Phase-2 playtest). --- PRIOR (A/B-ROUND-2 AUDIO RE-PASS, 2026-06-01). (1) SFX TIMBRE FIX: derived audio_system.sonic_character from concept.theme (soft organic acoustic — wooden/leaf/bell, no electronic/explosive) and regenerated all 3 SFX with it threaded into every prompt PLUS theme-aware negatives ('explosion, harsh, distortion, aggressive, electronic, synth, noise burst') that round 1 lacked — directly addresses the round-1 'SFX explosive, wrong for cozy theme' finding. (2) BGM-NOT-PLAYING — REAL ROOT CAUSE FOUND, supersedes round-1's wrong guess: round 1 blamed a same-frame play() after add_child; probing here proved that an IMPORTED long AudioStreamWAV (the ~30s bed) silently refuses to play (play() -> playing=false, pos=0) on the REAL WASAPI driver, while SFX (short) play fine AND a freshly-CONSTRUCTED AudioStreamWAV from the SAME PCM data plays. FIX (verified): _make_player rebuilds the bed stream in code from the loaded PCM data + loops the whole sample + autoplay before add_child; bgm.wav.import set to compress/mode=0 (PCM) so the rebuilt data is raw 16-bit (a QOA-compressed import would copy compressed bytes). Probe confirms MusicAmbient.playing=true with advancing get_playback_position(). (3) LEVELS: bed volume_db=-4 so it sits audibly UNDER the 0 dB SFX. Generated via Stable Audio Open (WAV, soundfile patch). Selftest still SELFTEST OK; headless run clean; game logic untouched. STATUS HELD (not auto-advanced to scored): awaits owner playtest + audio A/B. IP-safe: no artist/track names; music negative excludes vocals.

store_pass.notes: M2 foundation PROOF EXERCISE — tooling run against the validated substrate to confirm the seam executes + CI-checkable gate assertions pass. NOT packaged: creature-0001 is validated; both asset_pass + audio_pass are present but their owner A/B confirmations (and the cross-modal cohesion A/B) are still pending, and validated->packaged is illegal. icon_master derived from the styled spirit sprite; boot splash composites that master (60%) centered on a deep-forest bg via splash_render.gd; final icon/splash art is the deferred owner aesthetic A/B. APK build = the deferred Android-toolchain feasibility gate (ANDROID_HOME unset).
